updates.tweet_text:
Big Picture View: All month, #ES_F built a huge bull flag. June 10th, we held support. Monday, we hit resistance. 7590-7244=the flag. We could build for weeks

Plan Next Week: 7535/20, 7472 (watch traps)=supports. Fill out flag to 7590, dip, then breakout sees 7635, 7674, 7713+ https://t.co/emJs0CEo5F
updates.notes: 7674 and 7713 are new upside targets not in morning plan; 7472 named as support watch level, also new. 7535/7620/7635 are morning plan reaffirmations.

updates.tweet_text: @Redden843 Yes, thats absolutely true and I've spoken about that in the newsletter. Deep Failed Breakdowns tend to be dangerous unless the volatility is extremely high. 5-15 points is the sweet spot
updates.notes: Pure educational commentary about FBD volatility ranges; no specific actionable price level named.

updates.tweet_text: @badfutures40 @Redden843 Yes deep Failed Breakdowns also often end up being backtest shorts as well. If price loses a low by 30 points, it often just backtests it and heads lower. Needs to be very volatile/fast action to actually clear it and recover
updates.notes: Educational reply explaining FBD mechanics; no specific price named or actionable level introduced.
